# Input

A styled native input for short text and browser-supported input types.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Controlled and initial values](#controlled-and-initial-values)
- [Types and states](#types-and-states)
- [Debounced input](#debounced-input)
- [Accessibility](#accessibility)
- [Choosing an input](#choosing-an-input)

## Installation

Add the Input component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/input
```

## Usage

Use Input for a short, single-line value. Set a meaningful native `type`, `name`, and `autocomplete` where applicable so browsers can provide the correct keyboard, validation, and autofill behavior. A placeholder is an example, not a replacement for a visible label.

**Email input example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="w-full max-w-sm">
    <Field>
      <FieldLabel for="input-email">Email</FieldLabel>
      <Input id="input-email" type="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
      <FieldDescription>We will only use this for account notifications.</FieldDescription>
    </Field>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Field, FieldDescription, FieldLabel } from '@/Components/Field'
import { Input } from '@/Components/Input'
</script>
```

## Controlled and initial values

Bind `v-model` when other interface state depends on the current value. Use `default-value` for an initial value that can remain uncontrolled. The model supports strings and numbers, but native attributes such as `type="number"` still determine the browser interaction; normalize domain values at the boundary where your application needs stricter types.

## Types and states

Input forwards native attributes, so it supports types such as `email`, `password`, `search`, and `file`. Disable only values that cannot currently be edited. Read-only content should remain focusable with `readonly`. For invalid fields, set `aria-invalid` on Input and render a nearby explanation.

**Native types and states example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="grid w-full max-w-sm gap-5">
    <Field>
      <FieldLabel for="input-disabled">Disabled</FieldLabel>
      <Input id="input-disabled" disabled value="Unavailable" />
    </Field>

    <Field data-invalid>
      <FieldLabel for="input-invalid">Email</FieldLabel>
      <Input id="input-invalid" aria-invalid type="email" value="not-an-email" />
      <FieldError>Enter a valid email address.</FieldError>
    </Field>

    <Field>
      <FieldLabel for="input-file">Attachment</FieldLabel>
      <Input id="input-file" type="file" accept="image/*" />
    </Field>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Field, FieldError, FieldLabel } from '@/Components/Field'
import { Input } from '@/Components/Input'
</script>
```

## Debounced input

`DebouncedInput` delays `update:modelValue` while the user is typing. Use its `debounce` prop for search, filtering, or other non-critical work triggered by keystrokes, and `max-wait` to guarantee an eventual update during continuous typing. Clearing the field emits immediately. Do not debounce the local value of ordinary form fields or validation that users need as they type.

**Debounced search example**

```vue
<template>
  <div class="grid w-full max-w-sm gap-2">
    <Label for="input-search">Search</Label>
    <DebouncedInput
      id="input-search"
      v-model="query"
      :debounce="300"
      :max-wait="1000"
      placeholder="Search projects…"
    />
    <p class="text-sm text-muted-foreground">
      Emitted value: {{ query || 'None' }}
    </p>
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { DebouncedInput } from '@/Components/Input'
import { Label } from '@/Components/Label'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const query = ref('')
</script>
```

## Accessibility

Associate a visible label through matching `for` and `id` values. Keep instructions persistent when users need them to complete the field. Connect descriptions and errors with `aria-describedby`, and use `aria-invalid` only after the value has actually been evaluated.

## Choosing an input

> **Use Textarea for multiline content: [Textarea](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/textarea)**
>
> Use Textarea for messages, notes, descriptions, and other values where line breaks are meaningful. Input remains the right choice for short single-line values.

> **Use Input Group for prefixes, icons, or inline actions: [Input Group](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/input-group)**
>
> When supporting content belongs inside the input boundary, use the provided Input Group composition instead of absolutely positioning it over Input.

Hosted documentation: [Input](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/input)
